 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.8","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management (XDG + Schema Validation)

## Purpose
Centralize configuration so every command uses the same sources of truth for repos, hosts, secrets, and defaults. Consistent config prevents drift and makes automation reproducible.

## XDG Layout
- `~/.config/dsr/`
  - `config.yaml` (global defaults + schema_version)
  - `repos.yaml` (tool registry + build matrix)
  - `hosts.yaml` (build hosts + capabilities)
  - `notifications.yaml`
- `~/.cache/dsr/` (API cache, downloads)
- `~/.local/state/dsr/` (logs, state, run history)

## Precedence Rules
1. CLI flags
2. Environment variables (`DSR_*`)
3. Config files
4. Built-in defaults

## repos.yaml Schema (minimum fields)
- `tool`, `repo`, `local_path`
- `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (pre-release gates)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`

## hosts.yaml Schema
- host aliases, platform, capabilities, concurrency limits
- ssh options (connect timeout, identity)

## Migration & Versioning
- `schema_version` required
- `dsr config migrate` upgrades older schemas
- Unknown keys rejected unless `--allow-unknown`

## Commands
```bash
dsr config init
 dsr config show [key]
 dsr config validate
 dsr config set KEY=VALUE
 dsr config edit
 dsr config migrate
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads at startup with deterministic precedence
- [ ] Validation catches missing/invalid keys
- [ ] Defaults are sane and documented
- [ ] `--json` outputs resolved config for automation
- [ ] yq-based edits are safe; fallback mode is read-only","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:27.935807062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:12.840449840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:47:07.588658283Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr config command with init/show/get/set/validate/edit subcommands. Created src/config.sh module and main dsr CLI. XDG-compliant paths, JSON output support, precedence rules (CLI > env > config > defaults). Also implemented dsr doctor for diagnostics.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:56.274007404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:53.251178068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:48.532928914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.9","title":"Implement GitHub API adapter with caching + rate-limit handling","description":"# Implement GitHub API Adapter with Caching + Rate-Limit Handling

## Purpose
Centralize all GitHub API access so commands are consistent, cached, and resilient to rate limits.

## Responsibilities
- Prefer gh api when available (auth + pagination)
- Fallback to curl + GITHUB_TOKEN
- Support ETag / If-None-Match caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/
- Normalize errors into a common JSON shape
- Expose helpers for workflow runs, releases, tags, and compare endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unified helper used by dsr check/watch/release
- [ ] API responses cached with TTL + ETag
- [ ] Rate-limit errors trigger backoff + clear messages
- [ ] All errors map to global exit codes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413349290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413268548Z","close_reason":"Implemented GitHub API adapter in src/github.sh with: gh_api() for unified API access (gh CLI or curl fallback), ETag caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/, rate-limit handling with backoff, and helpers for workflow_runs, releases, tags, compare, and asset upload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2","title":"Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure","description":"# Local Build Infrastructure

## Purpose
Build binaries locally when GH Actions is throttled, using available hardware:
- Linux builds on Threadripper workstation (trj, local)
- macOS builds on Mac mini (mmini)
- Windows builds on Windows laptop (wlap) or cross-compilation fallback

## Build Hosts

### Threadripper Workstation (trj)
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64 (via cross), Windows (via cross)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun, Docker/act

### Mac mini (mmini)
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS
- Arch: ARM64 (Apple Silicon)
- Can build: macOS ARM64 (native), macOS x86_64 (via arch -x86_64)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

### Windows laptop (wlap)
- Connection: ssh wlap (via Tailscale)
- OS: Windows 10/11
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Windows x86_64 (native)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

## Cross-Compilation Strategy
For targets we can't build natively:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild or cross for Windows/ARM
- Go: GOOS/GOARCH environment variables
- Bun: bun compile with target flag

## Key Commands
```bash
dsr build ntm                       # Build for all targets
dsr build ntm --target linux-amd64  # Specific target
dsr build ntm --host trj            # Force specific build host
```

## Target Matrix (example)

| Tool | Language | Linux x64 | Linux ARM | macOS ARM | macOS x64 | Windows |
|------|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ntm  | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| cass | Rust     | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| bv   | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| ...  | ...      | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...     |

## Build Output
Artifacts + logs live under the state directory:
- `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/<tool>/<version>/<target>/`
- `~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/` (per-host logs + metadata)

## Dependencies
- SSH access to build hosts (mmini, wlap)
- Toolchains installed on each host
- Docker + act available on trj for Linux workflows
- Sufficient disk space (~5GB per full build)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host names and mappings align with `hosts.yaml` (trj/mmini/wlap)
- [ ] Artifacts and logs land in state directory with a manifest
- [ ] Cross-compilation paths documented for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Non-interactive builds run without prompts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:51:40.191796583Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2.1","title":"Implement dsr build command - local compilation","description":"# Implement dsr build command — local + remote compilation

## Purpose
Compile a tool locally or on remote build hosts, producing release-ready binaries with a manifest and logs.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr build <tool>
 dsr build <tool> --target <target>
 dsr build <tool> --host <host>
 dsr build <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr build <tool> --output-dir <dir>
 dsr build <tool> --parallel
 dsr build <tool> --resume
```

## Build Flow (deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
2. Acquire lock + create run_id
3. Select host per target (health + capability matrix)
4. Build via act for Linux jobs; native builds for macOS/Windows
5. Collect artifacts to state dir + manifest
6. Emit JSON summary

## Implementation Notes
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations (no global cd)
- Use `ssh <host> "<cmd>"` with explicit error capture
- Use rsync/scp with retries for artifact collection
- Use manifest schema as source of truth for release

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds all targets for Rust/Go/Bun tools
- [ ] Artifacts named per manifest contract
- [ ] Logs captured per host
- [ ] Parallel builds do not collide
- [ ] Resume skips completed ","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:28.345714914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:17:06.737036001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:26:47.825725483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:53.508442870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:37.866805709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.10","title":"Validate GoReleaser config compatibility for each tool","description":"# GoReleaser Config Validation

## Purpose
Ensure dsr can replicate GoReleaser outputs (targets, archives, naming) so fallback builds match official releases.

## Approach
- Prefer `goreleaser release --snapshot --skip-publish` when available
- Fallback: parse .goreleaser.yaml via yq
- Compare produced artifacts against expected naming template

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Targets and archive formats match GoReleaser config
- [ ] Naming templates produce identical asset names
- [ ] Fallback parsing works when goreleaser not installed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:21.603418246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:48.748847712Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:21.603418246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:49.305063723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.11","title":"Implement build workspace isolation + lock/state machine","description":"# Implement Build Workspace Isolation + Lock/State Machine

## Purpose
Prevent overlapping builds and enable reliable resume/retry by persisting build state per tool/version.

## Key Behaviors
- Acquire lock per tool+version (state dir)
- Create unique run_id + workspace (no overwrites)
- Persist state JSON with per-host status + artifacts
- Resume uses state to skip completed hosts

## Requirements
- Explicit error handling (no `set -e`)
- State updated after every host step
- Locks released on clean exit or timeout
- Stale lock detection (PID + timestamp + TTL)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent runs are blocked or queued deterministically
- [ ] State file created and updated per host step
- [ ] --resume continues from last known state
- [ ] Logs reference run_id + state path
- [ ] Lock files are atomic and auto-cleaned when stale","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024490854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024473411Z","close_reason":"Implemented build workspace isolation with lock/state machine in src/build_state.sh. 16 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.12","title":"Define artifact naming + build manifest schema","description":"# Define Artifact Naming + Build Manifest Schema

## Purpose
Standardize artifact names and produce a manifest JSON that release/install/tests can trust.

## Naming Contract
- `{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]`
- `checksums.sha256` includes all binaries
- Optional `.minisig` for each artifact

## Manifest Schema (minimum)
- `tool`, `version`, `run_id`, `git_sha`, `schema_version`
- `artifacts[]`: name, target, sha256, size_bytes, signature_path
- `hosts[]`: host, status, duration_ms, log_path, retry_count
- `build_env`: toolchain versions, workflow/job metadata, act version

## Placement
- `~/.local/state/dsr/manifests/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>.json`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest produced for every build
- [ ] Release + installer use manifest as source of truth
- [ ] Tests validate manifest schema
- [ ] Manifest includes toolchain + host metadata for auditability","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:45.903938160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:54:50.125203319Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:49:35.088175561Z","close_reason":"Created schemas/manifest.json (JSON Schema for build manifests), docs/ARTIFACT_NAMING.md (comprehensive naming convention docs), and scripts/tests/fixtures/manifest-example.json (test fixture). Defines {tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch} naming, manifest structure with artifacts, hosts, signatures, and SBOM references.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:45.903938160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.13","title":"Create act workflow compatibility matrix + overrides","description":"# Create act Workflow Compatibility Matrix + Overrides

## Purpose
Ensure each tool's release workflow can be executed locally via act with correct job names, runner images, and artifact paths.

## Deliverables
- Matrix: tool → workflow file → job names → act flags
- Known exceptions (jobs not runnable in act)
- Per-host overrides (linux/amd64 vs linux/arm64)
- `repos.d/<tool>.yaml` `act_job_map` entries for each tool

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each tool has an act-compatible mapping
- [ ] Unsupported jobs documented and skipped
- [ ] Artifact paths consistent across tools
- [ ] Overrides documented for non-standard workflows","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:52.346436490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:13:37.582165657Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:13:37.582147743Z","close_reason":"Created ACT_COMPATIBILITY.md matrix document and repos.d/ configuration templates (_template_rust.yaml, _template_go.yaml, ntm.yaml.example). Updated .gitignore for repos.d files.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:52.346436490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:31.371618554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.14","title":"Build from tag/commit for reproducibility","description":"# Build From Tag/Commit for Reproducibility

## Purpose
Ensure fallback builds are reproducible by building from the exact tag/commit that will be released.

## Behavior
- Resolve version → tag → commit SHA
- Support explicit `--ref <sha|tag>` override
- Use fresh workspace (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
- Record git SHA in manifest + logs
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations

## Git Plumbing (No Status Parsing)
- Dirty check: `git diff-index --quiet HEAD --` (exit code)
- Tag existence: `git show-ref --tags --verify refs/tags/<tag>`
- Resolve SHA: `git rev-parse <ref>`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds use tagged commit, not local HEAD
- [ ] Dirty working trees produce clear errors
- [ ] Manifest contains git SHA + resolved ref
- [ ] `--ref` supports both tags and full SHAs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:10.707996895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:31:08.268911076Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:31:08.268633734Z","close_reason":"Implemented git_ops.sh module with ref resolution, dirty tree detection, and build state git info tracking","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:52.253058133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:54.701273926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:10.707996895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:56.742094781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:59.320434061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.15","title":"Host selection + concurrency limits per host","description":"# Host Selection + Concurrency Limits Per Host

## Purpose
Choose the best host for each target based on health and enforce per-host concurrency to avoid overload.

## Behavior
- Select host using health + capability matrix from `hosts.yaml`
- Allow overrides: `--host`, `--max-parallel`, per-host concurrency caps
- Queue builds when a host is at capacity (FIFO with run_id ordering)
- Emit JSON details for scheduling decisions

## Design Notes
- Deterministic selection: same inputs → same host choice
- Never exceed per-host concurrency limits
- Record queue decisions in state/logs for postmortems

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host selection deterministic and configurable
- [ ] Per-host concurrency limits enforced
- [ ] Queue/defer decisions logged with run_id + target
- [ ] JSON output includes host selection rationale","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:17.923979053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:34.314246253Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:34.313966336Z","close_reason":"Implemented src/host_selector.sh with deterministic host selection based on health + capability scoring, per-host concurrency limits via lock files with stale detection, slot acquire/release API, and JSON output for scheduling decisions. Exports selector_choose_host, selector_acquire_slot, selector_release_slot, selector_queue_status.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:08.198973905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:17.923979053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:15.521885678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:13.167796475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.16","title":"Artifact retention + prune command (disk pressure guard)","description":"# Artifact Retention + Prune Command (Disk Pressure Guard)

## Purpose
Prevent disk exhaustion by pruning old build artifacts and cached downloads safely.

## Behavior
- `dsr prune --dry-run` shows what would be removed
- Retention policy configurable (keep last N runs per tool)
- Require explicit confirmation unless `--yes`
- Log every pruned path to state logs
- Hard safety: refuse to delete outside `DSR_STATE_DIR` and `DSR_CACHE_DIR`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Safe dry-run output
- [ ] Retention policy configurable
- [ ] Prune logs record what was removed
- [ ] Guardrails prevent accidental deletion of non-dsr paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:23.648918021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:26:58.834957229Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:26:58.834930970Z","close_reason":"Implemented prune command","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-11r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:46.986203142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:23.648918021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:23.490018340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:26.407796120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:41.648734026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:46.778018160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2.2","title":"Setup Linux build environment on Threadripper (trj)","description":"# Setup Linux Build Environment on Threadripper (trj)

## Purpose
Ensure the local Linux host (trj) has all toolchains and cross‑compile dependencies needed to build any tool deterministically.

## Host Details
- Alias: trj
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux (x86_64)

## Required Toolchains (trj baseline)

### Core Language Toolchains
- **Rust (nightly)** via rustup
  - `rustup install nightly && rustup default nightly`
  - Targets: `x86_64-pc-windows-gnu`, `aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu`, `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl`
- **Go (latest stable)** installed to `/usr/local/go`
- **Bun (latest)** via bun installer
- **Node.js (LTS)** via fnm or system package

### Cross‑Compile / Build Dependencies
- **cargo-zigbuild** (`cargo install cargo-zigbuild`)
- **Zig** (required by cargo-zigbuild)
- **MinGW-w64** (`x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc`)
- **aarch64 cross compiler** (`aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc`)
- **musl-tools** (if producing static binaries)
- **build-essential, pkg-config, libssl-dev**

### Build Orchestration
- **Docker** (for act containers)
- **act** (nektos/act runner)
- **jq + yq** (JSON/YAML parsing)
- **rsync + ssh** (artifact transfer)

## Verification (deterministic)
1. **Baseline report** using existing script:
   - `scripts/toolchain_check.sh --json` (captures Rust/Go/Node/Bun versions)
2. **Extended checks** (add to script or run manually):
   - `zig version`
   - `cargo zigbuild --version`
   - `x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc --version | head -1`
   - `aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc --version | head -1`
   - `docker info` and `act --version`
3. **Cross‑compile smoke tests** (use /tmp only):
   - Rust: compile a tiny "hello" crate for windows/arm64 targets
   - Go: `GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build` (hello)
   - Bun: `bun build --compile` for linux target

## Toolchain Harmonization Notes
- `scripts/toolchain_check.sh --commands` prints update commands per host; use **trj** as baseline version source.
- Do not create new directories under `/data/projects` for smoke tests; use `/tmp` only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Rust nightly installed + required targets present
- [ ] cargo‑zigbuild + Zig available
- [ ] MinGW + aarch64 GCC present
- [ ] Go/Bun/Node installed and in PATH
- [ ] Docker + act available
- [ ] `scripts/toolchain_check.sh --json` succeeds for trj
- [ ] Cross‑compile smoke tests succeed for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Verification output saved to logs (stderr) for audit

## Maintenance
- Run `scripts/toolchain_check.sh` weekly
- Document upgrade procedure for each toolchain","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:52.965747343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:30:29.552356671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:52.965747343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.3","title":"Setup macOS build environment on Mac mini (mmini)","description":"# Setup macOS Build Environment on Mac mini

## Purpose
Ensure the Mac mini (mmini) has all toolchains needed to build macOS binaries.

## Host Details
- Alias: mmini
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS (Apple Silicon ARM64)
- User: jemanuel
- Sudo: Passwordless via /etc/sudoers.d/jemanuel

## Required Toolchains

### 1. Homebrew (Package Manager)
\`\`\`bash
# Should already be installed, verify:
brew --version

# If missing:
/bin/bash -c "\$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
\`\`\`

### 2. Rust Nightly
\`\`\`bash
# Install rustup
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y

# Install nightly
rustup install nightly
rustup default nightly

# Add x86_64 target for universal builds
rustup target add x86_64-apple-darwin
\`\`\`

### 3. Go (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install go
# or download from go.dev for specific version
\`\`\`

### 4. Bun (Latest)
\`\`\`bash
brew install oven-sh/bun/bun
# or
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
\`\`\`

### 5. Xcode Command Line Tools
\`\`\`bash
xcode-select --install
# Needed for: clang, ar, ranlib, etc.
\`\`\`

## Universal Binary Support
macOS can build both ARM64 and x86_64:

### Rust Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build for both architectures
cargo build --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin
cargo build --release --target x86_64-apple-darwin

# Create universal binary with lipo
lipo -create \\
  target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/mytool \\
  -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

### Go Universal Binary
\`\`\`bash
# Build ARM64
GOARCH=arm64 go build -o mytool-arm64 ./cmd/mytool

# Build x86_64
GOARCH=amd64 go build -o mytool-amd64 ./cmd/mytool

# Create universal
lipo -create mytool-arm64 mytool-amd64 -output mytool-universal
\`\`\`

## Verification Script
Create ~/bin/dsr-verify-toolchains.sh:
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== DSR Toolchain Verification (macOS) ==="
echo -n "Homebrew: "; brew --version | head -1 || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Rust: "; rustc --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Cargo: "; cargo --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Go: "; go version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Bun: "; bun --version || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "Xcode CLT: "; xcode-select -p || echo "MISSING"
echo -n "lipo: "; which lipo || echo "MISSING"
echo "=== Rust Targets ==="
rustup target list --installed
echo "=== Done ==="
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All toolchains installed and in PATH
- [ ] Rust can build for both ARM64 and x86_64
- [ ] Go can cross-compile for both architectures
- [ ] lipo available for creating universal binaries
- [ ] Verification script exits 0

## Notes
- Mac mini is accessed via Tailscale, ensure daemon is running
- Apple Silicon can run x86_64 binaries via Rosetta 2
- Consider code signing for distribution (future enhancement)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:08.613890651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:43:42.553551067Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:43:42.553278974Z","close_reason":"Completed: All toolchains installed on mmini (Homebrew 5.0.12, Rust 1.93.0-nightly, Go 1.25.6, Bun 1.3.8, Xcode CLT). Universal binary creation verified for both Rust and Go. Verification script at ~/bin/dsr-verify-toolchains.sh.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:08.613890651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.4","title":"CRITICAL: Use nektos/act to run GH Actions YAML locally","description":"# Use nektos/act to Run GH Actions Workflows Locally

## Purpose
Reuse existing `.github/workflows/*.yml` to build releases locally when GH Actions is throttled.

## Key `act` Capabilities (from official docs)
- Run a specific workflow: `act -W .github/workflows/release.yml`
- Run a specific job: `act -j build-linux`
- List jobs: `act -l`
- Override runner images: `-P ubuntu-latest=...`
- Set container architecture: `--container-architecture linux/arm64`
- Control pulls / offline mode: `--pull=false` / `--action-offline-mode`
- Route artifacts: `--artifact-server-path /tmp/act-artifacts`

## Critical Limitations
- `act` runs workflows in **Docker** (Linux). It **cannot** execute macOS/Windows runner jobs.
- macOS/Windows builds must be native (mmini, wlap) or cross-compiled.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `act` runs `release.yml` for Linux targets
- [ ] Runner images pinned via `~/.actrc`
- [ ] Artifact collection path standardized
- [ ] Offline mode works once images are cached

## References
- https://github.com/nektos/act
- https://nektosact.com/usage/runners.html","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:00.354935621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:10:09.757102675Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:10:09.757085242Z","close_reason":"Created docs/ACT_SETUP.md, config/actrc.example, src/act_runner.sh with platform routing logic, and tests. Act integration ready for Linux builds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:00.354935621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.5","title":"Install and configure act on Linux host (trj)","description":"# Install and Configure act on Linux Host (trj)

## Purpose
Install `act` where Linux workflows will run locally. `act` runs Linux containers only and is required on the Linux build host.

## Linux Host (trj)
- Install Docker + act
- Configure `~/.actrc` (runner image, artifact path, container arch)
- Pre-pull runner images to avoid CI delays

## Optional: macOS Host (mmini)
- Install act via Homebrew if you want local workflow debugging
- Use Colima or Docker Desktop for Linux containers
- Set `--container-architecture linux/amd64` when running x86 workflows

## Important Limitation
`act` cannot execute macOS/Windows runner jobs; those builds remain native on mmini/wlap.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] act installed + working on trj
- [ ] Docker daemon accessible for act
- [ ] Runner images pinned and cached
- [ ] Artifact server path standardized (matches config/actrc)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:36.120915334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:39:59.135932462Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:39:59.135445605Z","close_reason":"Completed: act v0.2.84 installed, ~/.actrc configured, images cached, artifact server working. Verified with test workflows.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:36.120915334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:29.575978792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
